entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's platform that must implement the truth engine for professional assessment

entities.name: Triangulation
entities.context: Core principle from navigation and qualitative research; filtering noise by seeking intersection of multiple data sources

entities.name: Truth in professional assessment
entities.context: V's belief that valid professional data requires consistency across three planes

entities.name: Self-reflection
entities.context: First plane of professional assessment; prone to ego bias

entities.name: Interview performance
entities.context: Second plane of professional assessment; prone to performance/charisma bias

entities.name: External validation
entities.context: Third plane of professional assessment; prone to limited observation bias

entities.name: Truth engine
entities.context: Proposed Careerspan system that looks for convergence across three assessment nodes

entities.name: Matching algorithms
entities.context: Careerspan algorithms that should penalize divergence and reward consistency

entities.name: Trust score
entities.context: Metric based on integrity of the assessment triangle

entities.name: Multi-factor authentication
entities.context: Analogous mechanism using multiple verification sources

entities.name: Consensus algorithms
entities.context: Distributed systems concept analogous to triangulation

relationships.context: Triangulation is the underlying mechanism for finding truth in assessment
Self-reflection is one of the three planes where truth intersects
Interview performance is one of the three planes where truth intersects
External validation is one of the three planes where truth intersects
Careerspan must architect its truth engine based on this principle
Matching algorithms implement the triangulation assessment
Algorithms produce trust score based on triangle integrity
MFA uses same mechanism of multiple independent verification sources
Consensus algorithms use similar multi-source validation approach
